title: How query by objectID
body:
![](https://d3uepj124s5rcx.cloudfront.net/items/0n200o460Q2A0Y1j030I/Screen%20Recording%202017-03-01%20at%2009.34%20pm.gif?v=236e3467)

Hi,

One of the main user case for use instantsearch is for ecommerce. 

Under this scenario, instantsearch works really neat for get real time feedback. 

But this is one of a serie of steps to do after buy something. Normally, the next step is know more information about the item, something like, "hey I'm interested in this, tell me more".

This issue is related with this scenario. 

How to design a one product view for individual items?

we need to contemplate two scenarios variations for that:

- You are navigating in the App and decide know more about a item.
- You have a external link related with a particular item.

Also, we need a unique value. We are going to use `objectID` associated with each item in the index.

In the first case, when you are navigating in the app and decide follow a item, in this scenario, actually you have the item. Then you can control if show or hide the item in a dialog, as the Twitter example, an generate the absolute url.

The second case have a different precondition but basically the same. You need to define a `route` for fetch the item, something like 

```jsx
<Router history={browserHistory}>
        <Route path='/' component={component} />
        <Route path='/:objectID' component={component} />
      </Router>
```

And a way to connect the route with `InstantSearch` to say that the `objectID` is the criteria for get the item.

---

What do you think, guys? 
Could be possible with the current basecode, or need a extra component for that?

Also I think that this is a good approach for improve the SEO of a instantsearch application: Could be possible define a worker an create a `sitemap.xml` using all the `objectIDs` of the index.

Related: https://github.com/DeuxHuitHuit/algolia-webcrawler
